name: Hawaiian Sweet Bread
recipeIngredient: 2 packages active dry yeast (1/4 oz each)
4 1/2 to 5 cups all-purpose or bread flour
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, stir yeast into pineapple juice until dissolved.
2. Let stand until foamy, about 5 to 10 minutes.
3. Add sugar, potatoes, eggs, butter, salt, vanilla, ginger, and 2 to 2 1/2 cups of flour.
4. Beat at medium speed with an electric mixer for 4 minutes or beat 400 vigorous strokes by hand.
5. Stir in enough remaining flour to make a stiff batter.
6. Cover with a slightly damp towel and let rise in a warm place, free from drafts, until doubled in bulk, about 1 hour.
7. Grease two (9"x5") loaf pans or two (2 quart) casseroles; set aside.
8. Stir down the batter and turn into prepared pans.
9. Butter your fingers and two squares of waxed paper.
10. Smooth the batter with your buttered fingers.
11. Cover with buttered waxed paper and let rise until doubled in bulk, about 45 minutes.
12. Preheat oven to 375 degrees.
13. Bake for 30 to 40 minutes or until bread sounds hollow when tapped on the bottom. For browner bottom crusts, remove from pans and place directly on the oven rack during the final 5 minutes of baking.
14. Brush melted butter over tops. Cool on racks. Makes 2 loaves.
